title: Blocking fs I/O in async fns (watch.rs, db.rs)
description: watch_loop() calls std::fs::create_dir_all and std::fs::rename directly inside an async fn. init_data_dir() calls std::fs::create_dir_all and std::fs::write inside an async fn. These block the tokio executor thread for the duration of the syscall. Under load, or on slow/network filesystems, this stalls the entire async runtime. Fix: replace with tokio::fs equivalents (tokio::fs::create_dir_all, tokio::fs::rename, tokio::fs::write), or wrap in tokio::task::spawn_blocking.
close_reason: All pass 2 fixes applied: circuit breaker reset, persona-scoped 410, SSRF private IP block, tokio::fs, URL scheme validation, Content-Type, page clamp, watch dedup
